input.role: Newly appointed director of a national public health agency
input.context: Profile piece for a health policy magazine
input.story_angle: Whether his background in infectious disease research will shift the agency's priorities away from chronic disease prevention
input.known_facts: He has published extensively on pandemic preparedness. Previously held a university chair. Served on two government advisory panels.

input.role: Founder of a tech startup that just raised its first round of venture capital
input.context: Short profile for a business section

input.role: Chair of the Swedish government's AI ethics commission, appointed six months ago
input.context: Investigative piece examining whether the commission's recommendations have been influenced by industry lobbying
input.known_facts: She is a professor of philosophy at a Swedish university. She has published on AI ethics. Two of the commission's eight members have consulting relationships with major tech companies.
input.story_angle: Whether the commission is genuinely independent

input.role: A social media influencer with 4 million followers who has just been appointed as an unpaid advisor to the city mayor's office on youth engagement
input.context: Profile piece for a politics and media magazine
input.story_angle: Whether appointing an influencer to a quasi-governmental role blurs the line between public service and personal brand-building, and whether the appointment raises ethical questions about undisclosed commercial relationships
input.known_facts: Rivera has no listed educational background in public policy. Their income appears to come from brand partnerships. The mayor's press office has not disclosed whether Rivera will continue taking brand deals while serving in the advisory role. Rivera has 4 million followers on multiple platforms but their real name may not be Alex Rivera — this may be a brand name.

input.role: Newly appointed head of Nigeria's National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC)
input.context: Investigative piece for a pan-African health journalism outlet
input.story_angle: Whether her pharmaceutical industry background poses conflicts of interest given NAFDAC's role in regulating drug imports and combating counterfeit medicines
input.known_facts: She previously held a senior position at a multinational pharmaceutical company's West African division. She has a pharmacy doctorate from a Nigerian university. She has made public statements about modernizing drug regulation.

input.role: Executive producer of a streaming platform's new Spanish-language documentary division
input.context: Pre-production research for a podcast season about the business of streaming in Latin America
input.story_angle: How his background in telenovela production shapes his approach to documentary, and whether the division is a genuine creative investment or a market-entry strategy
input.known_facts: He produced several high-rated telenovelas. He recently relocated from Mexico City to Los Angeles.

input.role: Some kind of consultant, I think he works with the government maybe?
input.known_facts: Someone mentioned his name in connection with a planning decision. I don't have much else.

input.role: Chief Medical Officer of a private hospital chain that is the subject of a whistleblower complaint alleging unsafe staffing levels in intensive care units
input.context: Urgent investigative piece for a national broadcaster — the whistleblower's allegations will be covered by a competitor outlet within 48 hours
input.story_angle: Whether Hartley was aware of the staffing issues, whether she took action, and whether her public statements about patient safety standards are contradicted by internal documents the whistleblower has provided
input.known_facts: She has been CMO for three years. The hospital chain operates 12 facilities. She previously worked at a public hospital that was subject to a regulatory inspection. The whistleblower claims internal emails show Hartley approved staffing cuts.
